passages.title: Cosmetics in Ancient Past
passages.source_html:
Cosmetics in Ancient Past 


A 


Since cosmetics and perfumes are still in wide use today, it is interesting to compare the attitudes,customs and beliefs related to them in ancient times to those of our own day and age. Cosmetics and perfumes have been popular since the dawn of civilization; it is shown by the discovery of a great deal of pertinent archeological material, dating from the third millennium BC. Mosaics, glass perfume flasks, stone vessels, ovens, cooking-pots, clay jars, etc., some inscribed by the hand of the artisan. Evidence also appears in the Bible and other classical writings, where it is written that spices and perfumes were prestigious products known throughout the ancient world and coveted by kings and princes. The written and pictorial descriptions, as well as archaeological findings, all show how important body care and aesthetic appearance were in the lives of the ancient people. The chain of evidence spans many centuries, detailing the usage of cosmetics in various cultures from the earliest period of recorded history. 


B 


In antiquity, however, at least in the onset,cosmetics served in religious ceremonies and for healing purposes. Cosmetics were also connected with cultic worship and witchcraft: to appease the various gods, fragrant ointments were applied to the statuary images and even to their attendants. From this,in the course of time, developed the custom of personal use, to enhance the beauty of the face and the body, and to conceal defects. 


C 


Perfumes and fragrant spices were precious commodities in antiquity, very much in demand, and at times even exceeded silver and gold in value. Therefore, they were luxury products, used mainly in the temples and in the homes of the noble and the wealthy. The Judean kings kept them in treasure houses (2 Kings 20:13). And the Queen of Sheba brought to Solomon ‘camels laden with spices, gold in great quantity and precious stones.’ (1 Kings 10:2,10). However,within time, the use of cosmetics became the custom of that period. The use of cosmetics became widespread among the lower classes as well as among the wealthy; in the same way they washed the body, so they used to care for the body with substances that softened the skin and anoint it with fragrant oils and ointments. 


D 


Facial treatment was highly developed and women devoted many hours to it. They used to spread various scented creams on the face and to apply makeup in vivid and contrasting colors. An Egyptian papyrus from the 16th century BC contains detailed recipes to remove blemishes, wrinkles, and other signs of age. Greek and Roman women would cover their faces in the evening with a ‘beauty mask’ to remove blemishes, which consisted mainly of flour mixed with fragrant spices, leaving it on their face all night. The next morning, they would wash it off with asses’ milk. The very common creams used by women in the ancient Far East, particularly important in the hot climate and prevalent in that area of the globe, were made up of oils and aromatic scents. Sometimes the oil in these creams was extracted from olives, almonds, gourds, sesame, or from trees and plants; but, for those of limited means,scented animal and fish fats were commonly used. 


E 


Women in the ancient past commonly put colors around their eyes. Besides beautification, its purpose was also medicinal as covering the sensitive skin of the lids with colored ointments that prevented dryness and eye diseases: the eye-paint repelled the little flies that transmitted eye inflammations. Egyptian women colored the upper eyelid black and the lower one green, and painted the space between the upper lid and the eyebrow gray or blue. The women of Mesopotamia favored yellows and reds. The use of kohl for painting the eyes is mentioned three times in the Bible, always with disapproval by the sages (2 Kings, 9:30; Jeremiah 4:30; Ezekiel 23:40). In contrast, Job named one of his daughters ‘Keren Happukh’ – ‘horn of eye paint’(Job 42:14). 


F 


Great importance was attached to the care for hair in ancient times. Long hair was always considered a symbol of beauty, and kings, nobles and dignitaries grew their hair long and kept it well-groomed and cared for. Women devoted much time to the style of the hair; while not cutting, they would apply much care to it by arranging it skillfully in plaits and ‘building it up’ sometimes with the help of wigs. Egyptian women generally wore their hair flowing down to their shoulders or even longer. In Mesopotamia, women cherished long hair as a part of their beauty, and hair flowing down their backs in a thick plait and tied with a ribbon is seen in \ art. Assyrian women wore their hair shorter, braiding and binding it in a bun at the back. In Ancient Israel, brides would wear their hair long on the wedding day as a sign of their virginity. Ordinary people and slaves, however, usually wore their hair short, mainly for hygienic reasons, since they could not afford to invest in the kind of treatment that long hair required. 


G 


From the Bible and Egyptian and Assyrian sources,as well as the words of classical authors, it appears that the centers of the trade-in aromatic resins and incense were located in the kingdoms of Southern Arabia, and even as far as India, where some of these precious aromatic plants were grown. ‘Dealers from Sheba and Rammah dealt with you, offering the choicest spices...’ (Ezekiel 27:22). The Nabateans functioned as the important middlemen in this trade; Palestine also served as a very important component, as the trade routes crisscrossed the country. lt is known that the Egyptian Queen Hatsheput (15th century BC) sent a royal expedition to the Land of Punt (Somalia) in order to bring back myrrh seedlings to plant in her temple. In Assyrian records of tribute and spoils of war, perfumes and resins are mentioned; the text from the time of Tukulti-Ninurta Il (890-884 BC) refers to balls of myrth as part of the tribute brought to the Assyrian king by the Aramaean kings. The trade in spices and perfumes is also mentioned in the Bible as written in Genesis (37:25-26), ‘Camels carrying gum tragacanth and balm and myrrh’.

groups.title: Questions 15-27
groups.source_html:
Questions 15-21 


The passage has 7 paragraphs A-G. Which paragraph contains the following information? Write your answers in boxes 15-21 on your answer sheet.



Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
15

15 recipes to conceal facial defects caused by aging
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：An Egyptian papyrus from the 16th century BC contains detailed recipes to remove blemishes, wrinkles, and other signs of age.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落包含了关于隐藏因衰老引起的面部缺陷的食谱信息。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"recipes"和"facial defects caused by aging"来定位文章中相关的句子。

解题思路：在D段中，提到了一份来自公元前16世纪的埃及纸莎草纸，其中包含了详细的食谱，用于去除瑕疵、皱纹和其他年龄迹象。这与题目中询问的隐藏因衰老引起的面部缺陷的食谱信息完全吻合，因此D段是包含该信息的段落。
16

16 perfumes were presented to conquerors in war
✅ 正确答案：G

原文答案句：In Assyrian records of tribute and spoils of war, perfumes and resins are mentioned; the text from the time of Tukulti-Ninurta Il (890-884 BC) refers to balls of myrth as part of the tribute brought to the Assyrian king by the Aramaean kings.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问哪个段落提到了“香水被作为礼物赠送给战败的征服者”。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"perfumes"和"presented to conquerors in war"，在文章中定位到G段中提到"perfumes and resins are mentioned"以及"tribute brought to the Assyrian king by the Aramaean kings"，这与题目信息相符。

解题思路：G段明确提到在亚述的记录中，提到了战争中的贡品和战利品，其中包括香水和树脂，并且提到了由阿拉米国王带给亚述国王的没药球作为贡品。这与题目中“香水被作为礼物赠送给战败的征服者”的信息完全一致，因此答案为G段。
17

17 long hair of girls had special meanings in marriage
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：In Ancient Israel, brides would wear their hair long on the wedding day as a sign of their virginity.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是关于女孩长发在婚姻中具有特殊意义的信息。

定位关键信息：根据题目的关键词"long hair"、"girls"、"special meanings"、"marriage"，在文章中寻找相关描述。

解题思路：在F段中，原文明确提到"brides would wear their hair long on the wedding day as a sign of their virginity"，即新娘在婚礼当天会留长发作为纯洁的象征。这与题目中"女孩长发在婚姻中具有特殊意义"完全吻合，因此答案为F段。
18

18 evidence exists in abundance showing cosmetics use in ancient times
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Evidence also appears in the Bible and other classical writings, where it is written that spices and perfumes were prestigious products known throughout the ancient world and coveted by kings and princes.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问哪个段落包含“大量证据表明古代使用化妆品”的信息。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"evidence"和"cosmetics use in ancient times"定位到A段，该段明确提到"evidence"和"ancient times"的相关内容。

解题思路：A段首句提到"cosmetics and perfumes have been popular since the dawn of civilization"，接着明确指出"evidence also appears in the Bible and other classical writings"，并详细描述了这些证据如何证明古代人们重视身体护理和审美外观，与题目要求的"大量证据表明古代使用化妆品"完全一致。
19

19 protecting eyes from fly-transmitted diseases
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：Besides beautification, its purpose was also medicinal as covering the sensitive skin of the lids with colored ointments that prevented dryness and eye diseases: the eye-paint repelled the little flies that transmitted eye inflammations.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是哪一段落提到了保护眼睛免受飞虫传播的疾病。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"protecting eyes from fly-transmitted diseases"可以定位到E段中描述眼部化妆品用途的句子。

解题思路：E段中明确提到眼部化妆品除了美化外，还有药用目的，可以防止眼睛干燥和疾病，并且眼妆可以驱赶传播眼炎的小飞虫，这与题目所问的信息完全一致，因此答案为E段。
20

20 from witchcraft to beautification
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Cosmetics were also connected with cultic worship and witchcraft: to appease the various gods, fragrant ointments were applied to the statuary images and even to their attendants. From this, in the course of time, developed the custom of personal use, to enhance the beauty of the face and the body, and to conceal defects.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落包含了从巫术到美化这一转变过程的信息。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"from witchcraft to beautification"定位到文章中关于巫术和美化的描述。

解题思路：B段中明确提到了化妆品最初与巫术和祭祀有关，后来逐渐发展成个人使用的习惯，用于美化面容和身体。因此，B段包含了从巫术到美化的转变过程的信息。
21

21 more expensive than gold
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Perfumes and fragrant spices were precious commodities in antiquity, very much in demand, and at times even exceeded silver and gold in value.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问哪个段落提到了比黄金更昂贵的信息。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"more expensive than gold"（比黄金更昂贵）定位答案。在文章中寻找比较价格或价值的句子。

解题思路：在C段中，原文明确提到"Perfumes and fragrant spices were... at times even exceeded silver and gold in value."（香水和香草在古代是珍贵的商品，有时甚至比白银和黄金更有价值），直接对应题目中"比黄金更昂贵"的信息，因此答案为C段。

Questions 22-27 


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage 2? In boxes 22-27 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement is true FALSE if the statement is false NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage


22

The written record for cosmetics and perfumes dates back to the third millennium BC.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Cosmetics and perfumes have been popular since the dawn of civilization; it is shown by the discovery of a great deal of pertinent archeological material, dating from the third millennium BC.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问关于化妆品和香水的书面记录是否可以追溯到公元前第三个千年。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"written record"和"time"定位到A段中的"dating from the third millennium BC"。

解题思路：原文提到了公元前第三个千年的考古发现，但并未明确说明这些发现是书面记录。因此，无法确定题目中的"written record"是否在原文中有对应信息。所以答案为NOT GIVEN。


23

Since perfumes and spices were luxury products, their use was exclusive to the noble and the wealthy.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：However, within time, the use of cosmetics became the custom of that period. The use of cosmetics became widespread among the lower classes as well as among the wealthy; in the same way they washed the body, so they used to care for the body with substances that softened the skin and anoint it with fragrant oils and ointments.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问“由于香水和香料是奢侈品，所以它们的使用仅限于贵族和富人。”

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“perfumes and spices were luxury products”和“exclusive to the noble and the wealthy”定位到C段开头“Perfumes and fragrant spices were precious commodities in antiquity, very much in demand, and at times even exceeded silver and gold in value. Therefore, they were luxury products, used mainly in the temples and in the homes of the noble and the wealthy.”，再继续向下找到转折词“However”后的句子。

解题思路：题目说香水和香料是奢侈品，所以它们的使用仅限于贵族和富人。根据原文C段开头确实提到它们是奢侈品，最初主要用于寺庙和贵族富人家庭，但转折词“However”后的句子明确指出“随着时间推移，化妆品的使用成为当时的习俗，不仅在富人中，也在下层阶级中普及；他们像洗澡一样，用能使皮肤柔软的物质和芳香油膏来护理身体。”这与题目“仅限于贵族和富人”的说法矛盾，因此题目陈述是错误的。


24

In ancient Far East, fish fats were used as cream by women from poor households.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Sometimes the oil in these creams was extracted from olives, almonds, gourds, sesame, or from trees and plants; but, for those of limited means,scented animal and fish fats were commonly used.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是在古代远东地区，贫苦家庭的妇女是否使用鱼脂肪作为面霜。

定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词“ancient Far East”、“fish fats”、“cream”、“women from poor households”定位到D段，找到关于鱼脂肪作为面霜的描述。

解题思路：根据原文答案对应的句子可知，鱼脂肪是古代远东地区贫苦家庭妇女常用的面霜成分之一，因此题目的陈述与原文信息一致，答案为True。


25

The teachings in the Bible were repeatedly against the use of kohl for painting the eyes.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：The use of kohl for painting the eyes is mentioned three times in the Bible, always with disapproval by the sages (2 Kings, 9:30; Jeremiah 4:30; Ezekiel 23:40).

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是《圣经》是否多次反对用kohl给眼睛上妆。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"the Bible"和"use of kohl for painting the eyes"定位到E段，找到对应的句子。

解题思路：原文明确提到，《圣经》三次提到用kohl给眼睛上妆，并且每次都是贤者表示不赞同（disapproval）。因此，题目中的"repeatedly against"与原文的"always with disapproval"意思一致，说明《圣经》确实多次反对这种行为。所以答案是True。


26

Long hair as a symbol of beauty was worn solely by women of ancient cultures.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Long hair was always considered a symbol of beauty, and kings, nobles and dignitaries grew their hair long and kept it well-groomed and cared for.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问“长发作为美的象征仅由古代文化中的女性佩戴”是否正确。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“long hair as a symbol of beauty”定位到F段首句，该句明确提到长发是美的象征，并列举了国王、贵族和显贵也留长发。  

解题思路：原文明确指出长发不仅是女性的象征，男性如国王、贵族等也留长发，因此“仅由女性佩戴”的说法与原文矛盾，故答案为False。


27

The Egyptian Queen Hatsheput sent a royal expedition to Punt to establish a trade route for myrrh.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：It is known that the Egyptian Queen Hatsheput (15th century BC) sent a royal expedition to the Land of Punt (Somalia) in order to bring back myrrh seedlings to plant in her temple.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“埃及女王哈特谢普苏特派遣皇家使团前往彭特，目的是为了建立乳香贸易路线”，要求判断这个陈述是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：通过“Egyptian Queen Hatsheput”、“Punt”和“myrrh”这些关键词定位到G段末句，找到关于哈特谢普苏特派遣使团前往彭特的描述。

解题思路：原文提到哈特谢普苏特派遣使团是为了带回乳香幼苗种植在寺庙中（bring back myrrh seedlings to plant in her temple），而题目中声称目的是“建立乳香贸易路线”（establish a trade route for myrrh）。所以目的错误，故答案为FALSE。
